{{Quote|Neeka Sot is not a true warrior, but a member of an assassin sect shaped from birth for quick attack and close fighting. She did not best you in battle. Had I not stopped you, you would have killed her easily. She is also my personal bodyguard.|Harrar, to Khalee Lah|<ref name="Dark" />}}
'''Neeka Sot''' was a [[Yuuzhan Vong]] [[Gender/Legends|female]] from an [[assassin sect]] who served the [[Priest caste|priest]] [[Harrar]] as a [[Bodyguard/Legends|bodyguard]] in [[27 ABY/Legends|27 ABY]]. Disguised as a Yuuzhan Vong [[Warrior caste|warrior]], she accompanied Harrar on his [[priestship]] as he and the warrior [[Khalee Lah]] attempted to capture the [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Jedi Knight]] [[Jaina Solo Fel|Jaina Solo]] during the [[Yuuzhan Vong War]]. While in [[Hapes Consortium/Legends|Hapes Consortium]] [[Realspace/Legends|space]], Sot accompanied her master and Lah as they met with a pair of [[Hapan]] [[Pirate/Legends|pirates]] so that they could enlist their help in finding Solo. During the meeting, Lah tried to [[Death/Legends|kill]] [[Benwick Chell|one]] of the pirates as the warrior was contemptuous of the Hapan, but Sot stopped Lah from doing the deed at Harrar's request. She later killed [[Vonce|another]] pirate as Harrar bid her to do so.

==Biography==
{{Quote|Now go. Neeka Sot would be most displeased if she thought that my gift was unappreciated.|Harrar threatens Brenwick Chell with Neeka Sot to make him leave|<ref name="Dark" />}}
A [[Yuuzhan Vong]] [[Gender/Legends|female]], Neeka Sot was part of an [[assassin sect]], which trained her from [[Reproduction/Legends|birth]] to be proficient at quick attacks.<ref name="Dark" /> In [[27 ABY/Legends|27 ABY]],<ref name="Date">''[[The Essential Reader's Companion]]'' dates the events of ''[[The New Jedi Order: Dark Journey]]'' to [[27 ABY/Legends|27 ABY]].</ref> at the [[Time/Legends|time]] when the Yuuzhan Vong were [[Yuuzhan Vong War|invading]] a [[The galaxy/Legends|galaxy]] other than their [[Yuuzhan Vong galaxy|own]], Sot served as a [[Bodyguard/Legends|bodyguard]] to the [[Priest caste|priest]] [[Harrar]] aboard his [[priestship]], disguised as a common [[Warrior caste|warrior]]. That [[Standard year/Legends|year]], Sot and Harrar were near the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Myrkr/Legends|Myrkr]] in the priestship, as [[Warmaster]] [[Tsavong Lah]] had tasked Harrar and the warrior [[Khalee Lah]] with arranging the sacrifice of the [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Jedi Knight]] [[Twin/Legends|twins]] [[Darth Caedus|Jacen Solo]] and [[Jaina Solo Fel|Jaina Solo]]. The Solo twins had been a part of a [[Myrkr strike team|strike team]] composed mainly of [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]], which had been sent to the ''[[Baanu Rass]]'' [[Koros-Strohna|worldship]] in [[Orbit/Legends|orbit]] of Myrkr to destroy the threat of the [[voxyn]], who were able to hunt Jedi through [[The Force/Legends|the Force]]; the team was successful, though the Yuuzhan Vong forces under [[Executor (Yuuzhan Vong rank)|Executor]] [[Nom Anor]] captured Jacen.<ref name="Dark" />

Aboard the priestship, Sot brought Harrar a communications [[villip]], which he used to contact Anor about the status of the twins. From Anor, Harrar learned that Jacen had been captured, though Jaina and some of the strike force survivors had managed to escape aboard Anor's personal [[Starship/Legends|ship]], the [[Trickster|''Ksstarr'']]. After deducing that the survivors would head for the area of [[Realspace/Legends|space]] controlled by the [[Hapes Consortium/Legends|Hapes Consortium]], which was aligned with the remnants of the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]] in the war, Harrar and Lah directed the priestship to that area, and Sot accompanied them. [[Benwick Chell]] and [[Vonce]], deserters from the [[Hapan Royal Navy]] who had become [[Pirate/Legends|pirates]] and had also joined the [[Peace Brigade]], met up with the Yuuzhan Vong there in order the help them capture Jaina, and it was Sot who escorted the pair to her masters. Soon after the meeting's commencement, Lah became angered by the fact that the pair deserted, as desertion went against his beliefs, and he struck Chell in the throat, causing him to start choking. Lah would have then [[Death/Legends|killed]] the man, but at Harrar's signal, Sot prevented that from happening by quickly bringing the larger warrior to the ground.<ref name="Dark" />

The [[Assassin/Legends|assassin]] then knelt and offered her throat to Lah, so that he could kill her for the offense. However, Harrar saved her by convincing Lah, who was livid that he did not control all of the warriors, that the priest had ordered it. The pair of Yuuzhan Vong then listened as Vonce told them certain facts about the [[Solo family/Legends|Solo family]] and their involvement with the [[Battle of Coruscant (Yuuzhan Vong War)|fall]] of the world [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]] to the invaders, while Chell continued to choke. When Vonce was finished, Harrar had Sot kill the man by jumping on his back and breaking his neck. The assassin then cleared Chell's throat for him, before Harrar placed a [[tizowyrm]] in the deserter's ear to aid communication between the pirate and the Yuuzhan Vong. Upon Chell's departure after Harrar and Lah tasked him with capturing Jaina, Sot knelt before Lah because of the offense that she had committed against him, though Lah let her leave.<ref name="Dark" />

==Personality and traits==
{{Quote|You will be suitably rewarded.|Harrar, to Vonce, seconds before the priest has Neeka Sot kill the man|<ref name="Dark" />}}
Neeka Sot was adorned with [[Color/Legends|green]] and yellow [[Tattoo/Legends|tattoos]], and at the time of the meeting with the Hapan pirates, she was disguised as a warrior, wearing a mottled green set of [[Vonduun Skerr Kyrric|Vonduun crab armor]]. Sot had some notion of honor and, after bringing Lah to the ground, quickly knelt before him to let him take out his judgment on her, though Harrar saved her from the warrior's anger.<ref name="Dark" />

==Talents and abilities==
As an assassin, Sot was trained from birth to excel in close combat and in quick attacks, though according to Harrar, a warrior could supposedly easily take her down if he or she was aware of her. Sot was able to take down the much larger warrior Tsavong Lah, and she was able to easily kill the pirate Vonce.<ref name="Dark" />

==Behind the scenes==
Neeka Sot appeared in ''[[The New Jedi Order: Dark Journey]]'', a novel written [[Elaine Cunningham]] and published in [[2002]] as part of [[Star Wars: The New Jedi Order|''The New Jedi Order'']] book series.<ref name="Dark" />

==Appearances==
*''[[The New Jedi Order: Dark Journey]]'' {{1st}}
